title: Hierarchical Menu should expose whether an item has children
body:
**Feature ⚡️**

### What is your use case for such a feature?

Each item in a hierarchical menu component should expose whether any children will be displayed if the item is refined. In other words, whether further levels of refinement is possible or whether this is the last level. This **provides clarity** and **optimises the search process** for the end user. 

This is significant for discovery when a user doesn't know exactly how to categorise what they are searching for and is clicking through a large number of options.

Currently, when refining, a user will need to click into each level and only then do they discover if further refinement is available.

As an example, please see the screenshot below.

![Screen Shot 2020-07-17 at 3 22 49 PM](https://user-images.githubusercontent.com/880420/87745277-9c5d2180-c841-11ea-95db-a037eee2a666.png)

Ideally, we would like to add a caret (" > ") to the right of each item **that has children**. When the item is refined, the caret would turn downwards (we can do this using a custom template).

### What is your proposal

Update the object provided for each item to add a boolean `hasChildren` attribute, per https://www.algolia.com/doc/api-reference/widgets/hierarchical-menu/vue/?language=HierarchicalMenuList#scope

### What is the version you are using?

2.7.1
